Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Niiyama Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: categories]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - [[Category:completion tier 4]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}}

[[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}}

Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

[[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[ja:仁比山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Niiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮|12=draft}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]] [[ja:仁比山神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}}

The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]] [[ja:仁比山神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社|12=draft}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁|12=draft}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐|12=draft}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社|12=draft}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺|12=draft}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮|12=draft}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]] [[ja:仁比山神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}}

{{神社 |名称 = 仁比山神社 |画像 = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[佐賀県]][[神埼市]][[神埼町]]的1692 |ISO = JP-41 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 21|緯度秒 = 30.01 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 53.47 |ラベル位置 = bottom |祭神 = [[大山咋神|大山咋命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[天平]]元年（[[729年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 山王日吉宮、山王さん |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月26日 |神事 = 式年大祭（大御田祭）　 |地図 = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|仁王門]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|二の鳥居と楠]] '''仁比山神社'''（にいやまじんじゃ）は、[[佐賀県]][[神埼市]][[神埼町]]的にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[天平]]元年（[[729年]]）この地に[[松尾大社]]を勧請したことを創始とする。その後[[承和 (日本)|承和]]11年（[[844年]]）に[[円仁|慈覚大師]]が[[唐]]から戻る際この地に立ち寄り、土中より日吉宮の額を発見したことを朝廷に奏上したところ、[[比叡山|比叡]]の神威を感じた[[仁明天皇]]の命により[[近江国|近江]][[坂本 (大津市)|坂本]]の[[日吉大社|日吉宮]]の御分神を合祀し朝廷の[[祈願所]]とした。そのときにこの地を仁明天皇の「仁」と比叡山の「比山」を併せて「仁比山」としたとされている[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「仁比山神社」頁。（参考:[https://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7218014 JLogos]）[[#市史1|『神埼市史』第1巻]]、518-521頁,567頁。 従前は日吉神社、日吉社、また[[神宮寺]]として[[仁比山地蔵院|仁比山護国寺]]を併設し、山王[[権現]]、山王社と称していた{{R|角川1}}{{Cite kotobank|word=仁比山神社|encyclopedia=平凡社「日本歴史地名大系」|access-date=2023-12-26}}。参道一の鳥居は「山王権現」、二の鳥居は「日吉宮」の扁額{{R|角川1|市史1a}}。 国司や探題により社領や神田の寄進が行われたとされるが、文書の多くは兵火で失われている。残存する「仁比山神社文書」の古いものでは、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]16年興澄という者が[[神埼荘]]の屋地を寄進した記録がある。[[康正]]元年に[[大内教弘]]、[[文明 (日本)|文明]]17年に[[少弐政資]]がそれぞれ社領等を安堵している。[[永禄]]2年（1559年）[[大友義鎮|大友宗麟]]の来攻の際に社殿は焼失したが、[[佐賀藩]]藩祖[[鍋島直茂]]・初代藩主[[鍋島勝茂|勝茂]]親子の援助により再建された。江戸時代には、藩が営繕費用を負担し藩主が祭礼に代参を遣わすなど[[佐賀藩]]の庇護を得ており、大門（仁王門）前の鳥居は[[延宝]]2年（1674年）[[鍋島光茂]]が寄進している{{R|角川1|市史1a}}。 [[明治]]4年（1871年）に[[神仏分離令]]により仁比山護国寺と分離し日吉神社となった。この年村社に列せられたが、のち大正5年には県社に列せられている。1910年（明治43年）に[[仁比山村]]内の13社を、翌1911年（同44年）9月には村内の[[白角折神社|白角折社]]をそれぞれ合祀（[[神社合祀令]]）、同年10月に仁比山神社と改称した。なお、白角折社は後に旧地に復座している{{R|角川1|市史1a}}[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「仁比山村」頁。。 境内社として現在は松尾宮、松森稲荷神社を置く。 == 文化財 == === 無形民俗文化財 === * 「御田舞」（おんだまい）（「仁比山神社の御田舞」として佐賀県重要無形民俗文化財に指定、指定日:1959年3月20日）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2020-06-23 |accessdate=2022-12-26}} :12年に1度、[[申|申年]]の初申の日から13日間（4月頃）行う「大御田祭」の中で奉納される。[[平安時代]]に[[サル|猿]]を[[神使]]とする大津市の日吉大社から伝わった。当社および下宮である十禅師社にそれぞれ舞台を建てて行う。「御田歌」および演奏とともに種籾蒔き、田植えなどの所作が演じられる。豊作を願うもので、かつては[[口伝]]で受け継がれていたが現在は映像記録を利用している。御田役者と呼ばれる出演者は48名で、かつては申年生まれの男性が担った。神社と住民が組織した「御田舞保存会」が継承を担っている{{R|県1}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422174718/http://www.sankei.com/region/news/160413/rgn1604130008-n1.html |title=12年に1度、申年だけの豊作祈願 佐賀・仁比山神社で「大御田祭」 |publisher=[[産経新聞]] |date=2016年4月13日 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 |title=仁比山神社の御田舞 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 === 有形文化財 === * 金剛力士像（神埼市（旧神埼町）の指定文化財（重要文化財）） :参道入口の仁王門に安置されている、右が[[阿形]]・左が[[吽形]]の2体1対の[[金剛力士]]（仁王）像。共に高さ3メートル余りの木像で、胴体と頭は1本の[[クスノキ]]から切り出されている。直立に近い姿勢で腰帯が太く古様であることなどから制作時期は[[平安時代]]まで遡る可能性があり、九州でも最古級の仁王像となる。ただ、両腕は後世の補作で当初の作風とはやや異なった印象を与え、眉や額も中世に小材を貼り付けて補修されている[[#市史2|『神埼市史』第2巻]]、690頁{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmAboutDetail3.aspx |title=神埼市の指定文化財 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 * 仁比山神社文書（神埼市（旧神埼町）の指定文化財（重要文化財））{{R|市史1a|市指定L}}。 * 仁王門（[[佐賀県遺産]]） - 元来は[[仁比山地蔵院|仁比山護国寺]]の[[山門]]であったと考えられているが、現在は仁比山神社が管理を担う。[[寛文]]年間（1661-1673年）の建立と推定され、3間1戸の八脚門の特徴を持つ。古くは瓦葺屋根で、1980年（昭和55年）の改修で銅板葺きになった。もとは門前を通る[[佐賀県道21号三瀬神埼線|県道三瀬神埼線]]の道路上にあって、同県道の建設に伴って移設されている{{R|市史1a|市指定L}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=仁比山神社の仁王門 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/dynamic/isan/info/pub/Detail.aspx?c_id=90&id=107 |title=第2005-14号 仁比山神社の仁王門 （にいやまじんじゃのにおうもん） |website=佐賀県遺産データベース |publisher=佐賀県 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 === 天然記念物 === * 仁比山神社の[[クスノキ]]（神埼市（旧神埼町）の天然記念物）{{R|市指定L}} == 周辺 == [[File:Old house of Ito Genboku in Kanzaki.jpg|thumb|伊東玄朴旧宅]] 仁王門を経由して参道を上っていくと、右手の宅地の中の高台に伊東玄朴旧宅がある。宅地を過ぎると突き当たりの参道石段と右手の車道に分かれ、その間に神社駐車場がある。右手に進むと仁比山地蔵院でその奥が神社境内。石段を進むと左手に九年庵があり、突き当たって社務所下の石垣に沿い右に進むと広場に出て、その北の石段の先に仁比山神社社殿が鎮座する。 * [[仁比山地蔵院]] - 宮域の南に隣接。 * [[九年庵]] - 宮域の西に隣接。 * [[仁比山城]]跡 - 神社の北方に所在し、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]に主に[[九州探題]]側に属した山城[[#市史2|『神埼市史』第2巻]]、651-653頁。 * [[伊東玄朴]]旧宅 - 幕末-明治期に西洋医学の導入に貢献した伊東玄朴の生家。父の執行重助は仁比山不動院の被官を務めており、生家は神社参道の東側に所在する。佐賀県の史跡に指定{{R|角川1|市指定L}}{{Cite kotobank|word=伊東玄朴旧家|encyclopedia=平凡社「日本歴史地名大系」|access-date=2023-12-26}}。 * 十禅師社 - 仁王門の南東。日吉山王七社権現のひとつで、仁比山神社の下宮。大御田祭はここにも下り奉納される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=45 |title=かんざきを歩こう　散策マップ(1)《仁比山神社コース》 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *現地案内看板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=[[神埼市]] |year=2022 |month=3 |ncid=BC18582083 |ref=市史1 }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=[[神埼市]] |year=2022 |month=3 |ncid=BC18582312 |ref=市史2 }} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N-kIMmnjLO4&list=UUZSuCa33EbpljRBBLsYjulQ&index=16&t=0s さが祭時記まつりびと 仁比山神社の御田舞（神埼市）] - サガテレビ {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にいやましんしや}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:佐賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:佐賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:神埼市の歴史]] [[Category:神埼市の建築物]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]]

仁比山神社文書（神埼市（旧神埼町）の指定文化財（重要文化財））{{R|市史1a|市指定L}}。 * 仁王門（[[佐賀県遺産]]） - 元来は[[仁比山地蔵院|仁比山護国寺]]の[[山門]]であったと考えられているが、現在は仁比山神社が管理を担う。[[寛文]]年間（1661-1673年）の建立と推定され、3間1戸の八脚門の特徴を持つ。古くは瓦葺屋根で、1980年（昭和55年）の改修で銅板葺きになった。もとは門前を通る[[佐賀県道21号三瀬神埼線|県道三瀬神埼線]]の道路上にあって、同県道の建設に伴って移設されている{{R|市史1a|市指定L}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=仁比山神社の仁王門 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/dynamic/isan/info/pub/Detail.aspx?c_id=90&id=107 |title=第2005-14号 仁比山神社の仁王門 （にいやまじんじゃのにおうもん） |website=佐賀県遺産データベース |publisher=佐賀県 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 === 天然記念物 === * 仁比山神社の[[クスノキ]]（神埼市（旧神埼町）の天然記念物）{{R|市指定L}} == 周辺 == [[File:Old house of Ito Genboku in Kanzaki.jpg|thumb|伊東玄朴旧宅]] 仁王門を経由して参道を上っていくと、右手の宅地の中の高台に伊東玄朴旧宅がある。宅地を過ぎると突き当たりの参道石段と右手の車道に分かれ、その間に神社駐車場がある。右手に進むと仁比山地蔵院でその奥が神社境内。石段を進むと左手に九年庵があり、突き当たって社務所下の石垣に沿い右に進むと広場に出て、その北の石段の先に仁比山神社社殿が鎮座する。 * [[仁比山地蔵院]] - 宮域の南に隣接。 * [[九年庵]] - 宮域の西に隣接。 * [[仁比山城]]跡 - 神社の北方に所在し、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]に主に[[九州探題]]側に属した山城[[#市史2|『神埼市史』第2巻]]、651-653頁。 * [[伊東玄朴]]旧宅 - 幕末-明治期に西洋医学の導入に貢献した伊東玄朴の生家。父の執行重助は仁比山不動院の被官を務めており、生家は神社参道の東側に所在する。佐賀県の史跡に指定{{R|角川1|市指定L}}{{Cite kotobank|word=伊東玄朴旧家|encyclopedia=平凡社「日本歴史地名大系」|access-date=2023-12-26}}。 * 十禅師社 - 仁王門の南東。日吉山王七社権現のひとつで、仁比山神社の下宮。大御田祭はここにも下り奉納される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=45 |title=かんざきを歩こう　散策マップ(1)《仁比山神社コース》 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2022-12-26}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *現地案内看板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=[[神埼市]] |year=2022 |month=3 |ncid=BC18582083 |ref=市史1 }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=[[神埼市]] |year=2022 |month=3 |ncid=BC18582312 |ref=市史2 }} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N-kIMmnjLO4&list=UUZSuCa33EbpljRBBLsYjulQ&index=16&t=0s さが祭時記まつりびと 仁比山神社の御田舞（神埼市）] - サガテレビ {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にいやましんしや}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:佐賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:佐賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:日吉神社]] [[Category:神埼市の歴史]] [[Category:神埼市の建築物]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Niiyama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Niiyama Shrine hall of worship 2016-11-11 01.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
Worship Hall |location = 1692 Teki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=21|LatSec=30.01 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=21|LongSec=53.47 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = {{nihongo|Ōyamakui no Mikoto|大山咋命}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = Traditionally dated to 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) |Alias = Sannō Hiyoshi Shrine, Sannō-san |Festivals = April 26 (festival), Shikinen Taisai (Da-ota Festival every 12 years) |Map = Japan Saga }} [[File:Niomon of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Niō Gate]] [[File:Torii and camphor trees of Niiyama Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|Second torii and camphor trees]] '''Niiyama Shrine''' (仁比山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kanzaki Town, {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. Its historical Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine (県社). == History == According to tradition, Niiyama Shrine was established in 729 AD (Tenpyō 1) when the deity of {{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|ja|松尾大社}} was enshrined here. In 844 AD (Shōwa 11), {{ill|Ennin|ja|円仁}} (Jikaku Daishi) reportedly discovered an inscription of "Hiyoshi Shrine" upon his return from {{ill|Tang China|ja|唐}}, prompting Emperor Ninmyō to enshrine the deity of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} here as well. The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. The shrine was a site of devotion for local and imperial patrons and received land and offerings over the centuries, as documented in historical records.{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第2巻 (原始・古代、中世、近世編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} During the {{nihongo|Eiroku era|永禄|1558–1570}}, the shrine was burned down by forces of {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友義鎮}} but was later rebuilt with the support of {{ill|Nabeshima Naoshige|ja|鍋島直茂}} and his son {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]] [[ja:仁比山神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|仁比山神社|version=102285963|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The name "Niiyama" is said to derive from the emperor's name (仁) combined with the mountain's association with Hie (比山).{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1}}{{Cite book|editor=神埼市教育委員会市史編纂室 |title=神埼市史 第1巻 (自然・民俗・石造物編) |publisher=神埼市 |year=2022}} Previously called "Hiyoshi Shrine" or "Sannō Shrine," it was closely associated with {{ill|Niiyama Gokokuji|ja|仁比山護国寺}} and functioned as a {{nihongo|jingūji|神宮寺|Shinto-Buddhist shrine-temple complex}}. Following the {{nihongo|Shinto-Buddhist Separation Order|神仏分離令}}, the shrine was renamed Niiyama Shrine in 1911 after absorbing other local shrines. == Cultural Properties == === Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Ōta-mai (御田舞)''': A ritual dance performed during the {{nihongo|Da-ota Festival|大御田祭}}, held every 12 years in the {{nihongo|Year of the Monkey|申年}}. The dance involves 48 participants performing agricultural-themed movements to pray for a bountiful harvest.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |publisher=Saga Prefecture Cultural Database}} === Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Niō Gate (仁王門)''': Built in the {{nihongo|Kanbun era|寛文|1661–1673}}, the gate features two wooden {{nihongo|Niō guardians|仁王像}} over 3 meters tall. The gate was relocated during road construction in the 20th century.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=1032 |title=Niō Gate of Niiyama Shrine |publisher=Kanzaki City Digital Museum}} * '''Camphor Trees''': Designated as a city natural monument, these ancient trees are integral to the shrine's natural beauty. === Nearby Landmarks === * '''Niiyama Gokokuji''': A historic temple adjacent to the shrine. * '''Kyūnen-an''': A scenic garden west of the shrine. * '''Ito Genboku's Residence''': The birthplace of the 19th-century physician and pioneer of Western medicine in Japan. == Access == The shrine is a 15-minute drive from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=9313 Kanzaki City Digital Museum - Niiyama Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Niiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Hiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Niiyama Shrine]] [[ja:仁比山神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Niiyama Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|仁比山神社|version=102285963|comment=Imported full ja history}}
